dictionary_sections.definition: Source Universe Gap Review Packet is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{id0701}}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: Status: Review packet only; registry artifacts are internal evidence and need external/source span alignment before source truth promotion. ID range: ID0701 to ID0800 IDs with manifest records: 100 / 100 Manifest records in range: 4048 Usable records in range: 3448 Textual support candidates: 0 Formal proof records: 300 Wolfram records: 294 Gate counts: {"internal registry evidence needs external source alignment": 2854, "audit or build artifact not source prose": 600, "formal artifact parse before proof claim": 300, "wolfram artifact parse before symbol or runtime claim": 294} The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{id0701}}; \mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{id0701}}; \mathcal{Y}_{\mathrm{id0701}}; \mathcal{N}_{\mathrm{TRAWIN}}. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The supporting lemma is source locality: the selected relation appears in source_universe_gap_review_0701_0800_latest.md, and the adjacent context connects it to the title, domain, and surrounding argument. The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.

dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
